sourceMovie.title: Stepmom
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Stepmom for the same reason it broke through in 1998: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Chris Columbus keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Proposal
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Stepmom clicked for you, The Proposal (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Stepmom?
faq.answer: Try The Proposal, Husbands and Wives, Arthur—each shares DNA with Stepmom while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Stepmom?
faq.answer: Stepmom (1998) is best described as Drama, Romance, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Stepmom worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
